item.title: Texas Secretary of State Jane Nelson stepping down
item.description: Nelson’s office oversees elections. Abbott appointed her to the role in 2023 after she became the longest-serving Republican woman in Texas Senate history.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Contreras

item.title: Announcing our next round of can’t-miss speakers for September’s TribFest
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Eight data centers threaten to transform this small Texas county. Local officials say they have no power to stop them.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Martinez

item.title: Sid Miller to join Democratic nominee for agriculture commissioner at data center forum
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas lifts commercial drivers license ban for temporary agricultural workers
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: TribCast: Can Austin’s Project Connect get back on track?
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Victorious Paxton goes to Washington as former GOP foes open their doors — and wallets
item.description: The attorney general fundraised with Republican senators and met with rival-turned-ally John Thune, the Senate majority leader who had backed Sen. John Cornyn.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Screwworms are closer than ever to Texas, but not 1 mile away as lawmaker claims
item.description: On Monday, state Rep. Don McLaughlin said the parasitic fly that threatens the state’s cattle industry was 1 mile away. U.S. officials said that was not yet true.
item.dc:creator: Jayme Lozano Carver

item.title: Texas Children’s Hospital’s detransition clinic to launch this year, will include mental health and endocrinology services
item.description: The Houston hospital must set up the facility within 90 days of signing a settlement agreement with the Texas attorney general and and create a list of potential gender affirming patients, according to a document obtained by The Texas Tribune.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.

item.title: Texas schools say they still face budget difficulties despite $8.5 billion boost
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas’ app age verification law allowed to go into effect for now

Outside spending blitz defined the close of Texas’ District 35 Democratic runoff

item.title: Accused of a crime after shooting a migrant in Minnesota, ICE agent is arrested in South Texas
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Federal court allows Texas immigration law to take effect, continuing legal seesaw
item.dc:creator: Alex Nguyen
